verification.note: Reported by the TLDNN authors (Qu, Lu, Zeng, Wang, Wang, "Enhancing Automatic Modulation Recognition through Robust Global Feature Extraction," IEEE TVT 2024, arXiv:2401.01056), NOT reproduced or re-run by this repo. Figure is the plain TLDNN row in Table II: 62.83% average/overall accuracy on RadioML 2016.10a (11-class), averaged over the full SNR range -20..+18 dB -- the same dataset, class count, SNR range, and metric unit (accuracy_overall) as our board's AMC track, which is why this qualifies as 'from_paper' (not 'from_paper_uncertain'), mirroring the iqfm_paper.json / wirelessjepa_paper.json precedent: dataset+protocol identity confirmed, sample-level index overlap with our canonical split (amc-radioml2016-strat-snr-8010-seed42-v1) is NOT confirmed since the paper does not publish its exact train/test indices. Deliberately excludes the TLDNN+SS variant (63.35%, Table II/VI), which uses an additional 'Discrete Segment Substitution' data-augmentation scheme during training and is a distinct, separate configuration from plain TLDNN -- only the plain-TLDNN number is cited here. Paper states: "our proposed TLDNN model outperforms existing SOTA methods on Radio2016.10a across all SNR levels and achieves the highest average accuracy of 62.83% (+2.1%)" relative to MCLDNN (61.52%) in the same table. Verified directly against the raw arXiv HTML source (https://arxiv.org/html/2401.01056) via curl + grep/regex on the actual table cell content, not just a summarized WebFetch pass; per-SNR figures in the same row (58.3% at -6dB, 92.9% at 12dB) are consistent with the paper's narrative text, corroborating that 62.83% is the full-range average and not a single-SNR-point figure. The IEEE TVT venue-of-record PDF/DOI page (paywalled) was NOT independently checked; only the arXiv preprint was verified, which should match the published version but this has not been cross-confirmed against the journal's final typeset table. Weights/code for TLDNN are not confirmed to be published; this is currently UNIMPLEMENTED in this repo and represents the single highest-value reimplementation target on this task (would surpass our current top self_reported baseline, MCLDNN at 61.71%). Model size: n_params=243340 (high confidence — TLDNN paper (Qu et al., arXiv:2401.01056), Table II, TLDNN row 2016.10a Params = '243.34K'. Verified verbatim in raw arXiv HTML. INDEPENDENTLY corroborated by M…)
